task:
Ticket 0042 — align Deb's persona prose with the already-implemented read-only live terminal-observation path, and make the whole bundle land green.

CONTEXT YOU MUST RESPECT: The runtime for this ticket already exists as UNCOMMITTED working-tree changes and MUST be preserved exactly — do NOT revert, weaken, or rewrite it. It is: new file packages/core/src/runner/terminal-snapshot.ts (captureDebTerminalSnapshot + render), new test packages/core/tests/terminal-snapshot.test.ts, plus modifications to packages/core/src/runner/{observer.ts,prompts.ts,runner.ts,io.ts,index.ts,status.ts}, packages/core/src/index.ts, and tests prompts.test.ts/runner.test.ts/runner-direct.test.ts/runner-headless-bob.test.ts. This wiring writes local/runs/<id>/deb-terminal-snapshot.json each status refresh, threads terminalSnapshotPath into Deb's prompt (the 'How you see the run — terminal evidence first' section), and the DEB WATCH dispatch points Deb at the snapshot first. Treat all of that as correct and finished; your job is the persona prose + tests below, not re-doing it.

WHAT TO CHANGE (the only authoring this atom adds):
1. packages/personas/base/deb.md — the 'Observe run health' bullet (currently '... from the runner's status feed (it is your eyes — never probe panes or hunt run dirs)'). Rewrite so that for live-loop / stall diagnosis the runner-owned read-only terminal snapshot (deb-terminal-snapshot.json) is the DEFAULT first artifact, and deb-status.json is routing/summary/timestamp context — NOT the sole observation surface. Preserve the process-safety intent: Deb reads the runner-owned snapshot file; she still never starts/stops/focuses/closes/types-into or otherwise DRIVES cmux, windows, panes, sessions, or daemon lifecycle. Keep the 'You may observe Bob to diagnose but never direct Bob' authority rule intact.
2. cocoder/personas/deltas/deb.md — the bullet 'The runner maintains your live status feed (deb-status.json ...) and reads your nudge recommendations (deb-nudge.json). Use them — never attach to panes or scrape run dirs.' Reconcile it the same way: Deb reads the runner-owned deb-terminal-snapshot.json for live terminal evidence as the default, plus deb-status.json/deb-nudge.json for routing; keep the prohibition on ATTACHING TO / DRIVING panes and on scraping arbitrary run dirs by hand, but stop forbidding the sanctioned read of the runner-owned snapshot. Do not contradict the base prose.
3. packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md — the 'Host And Process Safety' section. Add ONE tight clause that reading runner/session-host-owned read-only artifacts (e.g. the terminal snapshot) is permitted and is distinct from driving cmux/windows/panes/daemon lifecycle, which remains forbidden. Keep it minimal — do not restructure the section.

DEFECT-CLASS CHECK: I grepped the live tree; the only two prose sites still asserting the old 'status feed is your only eyes / never probe panes' model are base/deb.md:24 and deltas/deb.md:31. If your own grep (your eyes|never probe panes|hunt run dirs|attach to panes|scrape run dirs) surfaces any additional site, fix it too and tell me.

ACCEPTANCE / EVIDENCE (report exit codes, do not weaken any test to pass):
- Run the core package test suite (e.g. pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder/packages/core' test) INCLUDING terminal-snapshot.test.ts, prompts.test.ts, and runner.test.ts; all green.
- Run any persona/prompt validation suite that pins Deb's prompt or persona frontmatter; all green.
- Run typecheck for packages/core.
- Confirm by reading the rendered text that base/deb.md no longer says the status feed is Deb's only eyes and that prompts.test.ts still asserts the terminal-evidence-first section.
- Do NOT touch product code outside the persona/standards files except as strictly needed; the uncommitted runtime stays as-is.
Report: files changed, the exact commands run with their exit codes, and any extra defect-class site found.
